title: Ketan murder case: Police turn to gait analysis, hunt for clothes Chetan wore that day
content: Police are employing forensic gait analysis to verify claims of accused Chetan Chaudhary being near Lohagad Fort on the day of Ketan Agrawal's death. A fresh crime scene reconstruction with Chaudhary is planned, alongside efforts to recover his alleged attire. Both Chaudhary and co-accused Siya Goyal are in police custody until July 3 as the investigation into the alleged murder continues.

title: Congress delegation Ayodhya visit row: Yogi Adityanath slams party, says it is 'leaning on lies'
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath sharply criticized the Congress over the Ram Temple donation row, accusing them of hypocrisy and past anti-devotee actions. He stated that those who once opposed Lord Ram are now eager to visit Ayodhya. This comes as the Congress alleged house arrests of its leaders, including state chief Ajay Rai, who planned to visit Ayodhya amid the embezzlement controversy.

title: Nitish Reddy racing against time to be fit for England ODIs; Gill, Rahul to reach early
content: All-rounder Nitish Kumar Reddy faces a race against time for the upcoming ODI series against England, with a minor left quadricep pull sidelining him. While the management is optimistic about his return next month, his participation in England appears unlikely. The Zimbabwe T20Is are being considered as a potential comeback opportunity.

title: Elon Musk agrees with what Apple CEO Tim Cook said has shocked him; says: Biggest ...
content: Tech giants like Apple and Microsoft are significantly increasing prices for devices like iPads, MacBooks, and Xbox consoles due to a severe global memory chip shortage. This surge, driven by the AI boom's demand for data centers, has quadrupled memory costs. Apple stated they absorbed these increases for as long as possible before passing them on to consumers.

title: Jio IPO: What is Project Jupiter? How Mukesh Ambani’s Reliance worked behind closed doors for India's biggest offering
content: Reliance engaged with regulators to secure greater flexibility in IPO rules, encouraging key investors to divest their holdings, and designing the country's largest public offering while keeping its structure under wraps. The highly confidential initiative was internally code-named Project Jupiter, reflecting both its scale and strategic importance.

title: Can’t afford a lawyer? Who can get free legal aid in India and how to apply
content: Accessing justice in India is now easier for those who cannot afford legal representation. The National Legal Services Authority (NALSA) offers free lawyers and legal assistance to specific vulnerable groups, including women, children, and individuals from Scheduled Castes/Tribes, irrespective of income. Others can qualify based on income thresholds, which vary by court level and state. Applications can be submitted in person or online, with decisions made swiftly.

title: Why Klopp targeted Arsenal while slamming VAR after Germany’s World Cup exit
content: Germany's World Cup exit has ignited fierce debate, with Jurgen Klopp leading criticism of a disallowed extra-time winner. Klopp questioned VAR's interpretation of fouls, even referencing Arsenal's set-piece success. Legends Miroslav Klose and Bastian Schweinsteiger echoed these sentiments, lamenting VAR's perceived overreach and its impact on player psychology, particularly after Jonathan Tah missed a crucial penalty.

title: '994 days of genocide': Microsoft engineer quits, emails thousands on his final day
content: A Microsoft data center technician in Italy resigned on his final day, emailing thousands of colleagues across Europe to accuse the company of powering Israel's war in Gaza through its Azure cloud and European data centers. Published by No Azure for Apartheid, the email cites the Unit 8200 surveillance exposé and urges Microsoft workers to "reclaim our labor" and divest.

title: Russia's 28,000-tonne nuclear cruiser is back at sea — and Nato will be watching
content: After nearly thirty years undergoing substantial renovations, Russia's Kirov-class missile cruiser, the Admiral Nakhimov, is back in action. This nuclear-powered giant, featuring cutting-edge missile systems and superior air defense capabilities, has been deployed along critical NATO shipping lanes in the Arctic. The cruiser’s return represents a major increase in Russia's maritime strength, aimed at asserting dominance in important northern regions.

title: 'Stripped, thrashed': Bengaluru SI forces minor boys to perform obscene acts, records videos
content: A Bengaluru police sub-inspector, Praveen, has been arrested for allegedly sexually harassing and assaulting minor boys during a murder inquiry. Sources claim he thrashed and stripped the boys, forcing them into obscene acts and recording them. The leaked video prompted a suo-motu case under the POCSO Act, leading to his apprehension and custody for questioning.

title: Strait back in business: Hormuz traffic picks up as supertankers slide through passage
content: The Strait of Hormuz is seeing a welcome return of maritime traffic after a period of intense tension. Following recent attacks and subsequent retaliatory strikes, crude carriers are cautiously re-entering the Persian Gulf. Data indicates a significant increase in commodity vessel transits, signaling renewed confidence among shipowners and a potential boost for regional oil output.

title: WhatsApp username feature launched: What it is, how to reserve and more
content: WhatsApp is rolling out a new username feature, allowing users to connect without sharing phone numbers. This privacy upgrade lets you reserve a unique username, similar to social media handles, to protect your personal contact details.  The company aims to provide a safer way for its over three billion users to interact with new people, with an optional 'username key' for added control over who can message you.

title: 'He was playing for himself': Srikkanth blasts Tilak after series defeat vs Ireland
content: Former India captain Krishnamachari Srikkanth has slammed Tilak Varma and other Indian batters following a shocking one-run defeat to Ireland. Srikkanth criticized their slow scoring and lack of attacking intent in the middle overs, stating that such an approach is unacceptable in T20 cricket. He highlighted a period of 45 balls without a boundary as particularly concerning, leading to India's first-ever series loss against Ireland in the format.

title: Tenant has sublet the property—can you evict them? Here’s the legal route explained
content: Landlords face a strict legal path when tenants sublet properties against lease terms. Self-help measures like changing locks are illegal and can lead to liability. Eviction requires formal proceedings through rent control authorities or civil courts, backed by solid evidence of unauthorized subletting. Tenants must obtain written consent for any subletting arrangements, as prohibited subletting is a recognized ground for eviction.

title: Armed forces see major leadership reshuffle as new commanders take charge
content: A significant leadership overhaul has swept through India's armed forces, with new chiefs appointed for the Army, Navy, and Defence Staff. Key Army and Air Force positions have also seen a change of guard. These appointments bring seasoned operational and strategic expertise to the forefront, ensuring continuity and readiness as the military confronts evolving security landscapes and modernization efforts.

title: Businessman's son Ayush Malik, who became Mohammad Ali, returns to Hinduism, cites this reason
content: A young man, Ayush Malik, who had publicly converted to Islam and changed his name, has now returned to Hinduism. A video shows him performing puja and expressing remorse to his mother, stating his decision was voluntary after witnessing his family's distress. His earlier conversion and marriage had led to controversy and legal action under Uttar Pradesh's anti-conversion law.

title: Cowboys linked to $53M Colts star in blockbuster NFL trade proposal
content: The Dallas Cowboys have been named a potential landing spot for Indianapolis Colts star Jonathan Taylor in a proposed trade. CBS Sports' Garrett Podell suggested the All-Pro running back could be available for a third-round pick if the Colts struggle in 2026. With Taylor entering the final year of his contract and coming off another standout season, Dallas could pursue him if its rushing attack fails to meet expectations.

title: Brittany Mahomes shares heartfelt promise inspired by Patrick Mahomes
content: Brittany Mahomes says becoming a mother has made her more ambitious and intentional about pursuing her goals. She hopes her children, Sterling, Bronze, and Golden, see both her and Patrick Mahomes working hard and following their dreams. Brittany believes leading by example will teach them that success comes through determination and perseverance. She also shared that her daughter Sterling has shown growing interest in her work, making those moments even more meaningful.

title: 'Name on letterhead doesn’t matter': US envoy Gor on Indo-Pacific Command renaming row
content: US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor, dismissed concerns over the renaming of the Indo-Pacific Command, emphasizing that the "name on the letterhead" is less important than actual actions. He highlighted that India continues to conduct more military exercises with the US than any other nation, underscoring the robust and strong footing of bilateral ties across trade, defense, and people-to-people connections.

title: ‘Holiday, damn it!’: Paraguay president calls national celebration after shocking Germany
content: Paraguay's stunning World Cup upset over four-time champions Germany, securing a 4-3 penalty shootout victory after a 1-1 draw, has prompted President Santiago Peña to declare a national holiday. The dramatic Round of 32 match saw a controversial VAR decision overturn a late German goal, paving the way for Paraguay's historic win.

title: Pakistan backtracks on energy gamble, buys emergency LNG as Qatar exports stay in limbo
content: Pakistan has urgently secured a liquefied natural gas shipment amid ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz impacting Qatari exports.  The nation's energy supply chain faces strain due to regional tensions, forcing reliance on costly spot market purchases.  These incidents, including attacks on vessels, have halted Qatar's LNG flows, creating global energy market volatility and exacerbating Pakistan's economic pressures, including rising inflation.

title: FIR filed against cricketer Shashank Singh, father after cook alleges assault and abuse
content: Cricketer Shashank Singh and his father, retired police official Shailesh Singh, face charges in Bhopal after a domestic cook accused them of assault, abuse, and confiscating his mobile phone. The cook alleges he was forced out of the bungalow and had money deducted from his phone for food and accommodation. An investigation is underway following the FIR.

title: ‘We feel used and cheated’: Muller slams VAR after Germany’s World Cup heartbreak
content: Germany's World Cup dreams were shattered by a controversial VAR decision that disallowed Jonathan Tah's extra-time goal against Paraguay. Thomas Muller vehemently criticised the technology, expressing deep frustration over the "heartbreaking" erasure of a moment that Germany believed secured their victory. The match ultimately went to penalties, where Germany suffered a shock exit.

title: Kannada actress Divya Suresh alleges late-night harassment in Bengaluru streets
content: Kannada actress Divya Suresh and her cousin were subjected to harassment by an unidentified man late Sunday night in Bengaluru. The actor recounted the traumatic experience on social media, detailing how the man followed them and made obscene gestures, raising serious concerns about women's safety in the city. Suresh questioned the public safety assurances, tagging the Bengaluru Police.

title: The Rs 40 lakh house hunt: Which metro gives you the best deal?
content: Finding a home under Rs 40 lakh in India's major cities is becoming a challenge, with only 10% of new launches falling into this category. Kolkata offers the best prospects, boasting 41% of its new supply below this price point. In contrast, cities like NCR and Bengaluru are dominated by luxury projects, making affordable options scarce for budget-conscious buyers.

title: Gold price prediction: Why prices are unlikely to rise much in near-term; check outlook
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are expected to continue being under pressure in the near-term as rate hike fears feed into a stronger dollar, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: 'You may face difficulties with govt schemes if ...': DKS's warning as Karnataka SIR begins
content: Karnataka Chief Minister DK Shivakumar urged citizens to register their names during the special electoral roll revision, emphasizing that failure to do so could jeopardize access to crucial government welfare schemes like Gruha Jyothi and Gruha Lakshmi. The revision, running from June 30 to July 29, aims to update voter details, with assistance available through help desks and party representatives.

title: AP EAPCET result 2026: Know result time and how to check rank card
content: AP EAMCET 2026 result: The APSCHE is expected to announce the AP EAMCET 2026 results on June 30 at its official admissions portal. Candidates can download their scorecards and rank cards using their registration details. General category candidates need at least 40 out of 160 marks to qualify, while no minimum marks apply for SC and ST candidates. Qualified candidates can participate in the counselling process, which includes registration, document verification, choice filling, seat allotment and admission confirmation.

title: 'I'm throwing my 100% support': Stokes names Brook as preferred successor
content: Ben Stokes has unequivocally backed Harry Brook as his successor to captain England's Test side. Despite a recent decision to appoint Joe Root in Stokes' absence, the retiring all-rounder emphasized Brook's vice-captaincy as a clear indicator of his readiness. Stokes believes Brook's leadership skills and growing influence make him the natural choice to guide the team's future, offering his full support for the Yorkshire batter.

title: INDIA bloc leaders write to CJI over EC’s SIR process; AAP, DMK come on board to sign joint letter
content: INDIA bloc parties have formally petitioned the Chief Justice of India regarding the Election Commission's special intensive revision of electoral rolls and other election-related concerns. This joint letter, signed by 23 parties and an independent, follows a recent opposition meeting where consensus was reached on key issues. The alliance emphasized solidarity and resistance, aiming to address perceived electoral irregularities and demanding the Education Minister's resignation over examination controversies.

title: How Ram Temple donations were received, counted & deposited: Cops quiz Champat Rai
content: Ayodhya police have intensified their probe into alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple donations, recording statements from former Trust general secretary Champat Rai and other functionaries. Investigators are meticulously examining the entire donation management process, from receipt to deposit, to identify procedural lapses and establish accountability. This crucial step aims to reconstruct the movement of funds and uncover any conspiracy, with financial records and evidence being corroborated.

title: ‘BMC must wake up now’: Bombay HC raps civic body over potholes, open manholes
content: Mumbai's roads and open manholes have drawn sharp criticism from the Bombay High Court, with judges expressing dismay over the two-decade-long problem. The court lambasted the BMC for its inaction, urging them to learn from international road construction standards. Additionally, the Thane Municipal Corporation faced reprimand for delaying compensation to a doctor's family after his death due to a manhole incident. The court demands concrete action and accountability.

title: Saibari’s winner sends Morocco into last 16 as Netherlands crash to earliest WC exit
content: Morocco stunned the Netherlands in a dramatic penalty shootout, securing a 3-2 victory after a 1-1 draw to advance to the last 16 of the FIFA World Cup. Goalkeeper Yassine Bounou's crucial save paved the way for Ismael Saibari's winning penalty. The Atlas Lions fought back from Cody Gakpo's late goal, forcing extra time with a stoppage-time header, ending the Dutch's World Cup campaign.

title: Bengal slashes 65 Muslim sub-groups from TMC-era list, clears twin OBC quota bills
content: West Bengal's assembly has passed two bills to revise the OBC reservation framework, now recognizing 66 communities (54 Hindu, 12 Muslim) outside SC/ST categories. This move reverses changes made by the previous TMC government, which had faced legal challenges. The new legislation restores greater power to the West Bengal Commission for Backward Classes, making its recommendations binding on the government for determining reservation percentages and community classifications.

title: India announce Harmanpreet-led squad for Asian Games after T20 World Cup exit
content: India's Women's Selection Committee has unveiled the squad for the 2026 Asian Games in Japan, where they aim to defend their gold medal title. Led by Harmanpreet Kaur, the team features a blend of seasoned stars and emerging talent, including Smriti Mandhana as vice-captain. The squad announcement sets the stage for another strong showing by the Indian women's cricket team on the international stage.

title: General Dhiraj Seth set to take over as the 31st Chief of the Army Staff
content: General Dhiraj Seth assumes command as the Army's 31st Chief of Army Staff, succeeding General Upendra Dwivedi. A distinguished Armoured Corps officer, General Seth brings extensive command experience from Southern and South Western Commands, and has overseen crucial staff roles in modernization. His tenure is set to focus on the army's 'decade of transformation,' including integrated battle groups and drone integration, aiming to enhance inter-service coordination.

title: HbA1c vs daily glucose monitoring: Doctor explains which number really matters
content: Millions managing diabetes rely on morning blood sugar checks, but this single number misses crucial fluctuations.  HbA1c offers a two-to-three-month average, revealing a broader picture.  However, a good HbA1c doesn't guarantee stable control, and individual glucose patterns vary significantly. Experts emphasize a holistic approach, combining daily readings with HbA1c and other data for effective diabetes management and to mitigate long-term complications.

title: Massive fire breaks out at Bengal's Haldia refinery, several workers injured
content: A significant fire erupted at the Petrochemical Complex in West Bengal early Tuesday morning, engulfing a naphtha-carrying pipeline. Several workers sustained burn injuries and were promptly taken for medical attention. The exact cause of the blaze remains under investigation. Details regarding the full extent of the damage and the condition of those injured are still emerging.

title: Delhi EV policy keeps hybrids out, includes 3-year lock-in period
content: Delhi's new EV policy, effective July 1, prioritizes pure electric vehicles, excluding hybrids. It offers substantial road tax and registration fee exemptions for electric cars under Rs 30 lakh, alongside a Rs 1 lakh incentive for scrapping old cars to buy new EVs. Subsidies are also available for electric two and three-wheelers, aiming to significantly curb pollution and boost clean mobility.

title: 'Afraid of Ram devotees coming?' Congress claims leaders under house arrest ahead of Ayodhya visit
content: Ajay Rai, the Congress chief in Uttar Pradesh, has accused the BJP government of placing him under house arrest to stop a party delegation from reaching the revered Ram Temple in Ayodhya. He suggested that the government is anxious about a potential backlash from devotees regarding a donation embezzlement scandal. Rai's wife voiced her concerns, stating that the BJP would be accountable for any harm that might come to him.

title: How 'future prospects' helped a family secure higher accident compensation
content: Andhra Pradesh High Court significantly boosted compensation for a road accident victim's family, raising it from Rs 7.28 lakh to Rs 10.06 lakh. The court found the tribunal underestimated the deceased credit officer's future earnings. Rejecting the APSRTC's claims of contributory negligence, the court emphasized fair compensation, even increasing the award suo motu to account for lost companionship and estate.

title: India’s economy could hit $7 trillion this decade, double by mid-2030s
content: India's economy is projected to reach $7 trillion by 2030, with ambitions for $14 trillion by mid-2030s and $25-30 trillion by 2047, according to Ambassador Vinay Mohan Kwatra.  Meanwhile, the US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor, expressed optimism about finalizing an interim trade deal soon and hinted at a potential visit by President Trump to India next year, underscoring strengthening bilateral ties.

title: 'Once-in-a-generation player': Latham, Root pay heartfelt tributes to Stokes
content: England's star all-rounder Ben Stokes has bid farewell to Test cricket, announcing his retirement after a memorable career. Despite a loss in his final match against New Zealand, Stokes received glowing tributes from opponents and teammates. New Zealand captain Tom Latham lauded him as a "once-in-a-generation player," while Joe Root reflected on their long-standing friendship and the void Stokes' absence will leave in the dressing room.

title: Monsoon arrived but rain didn’t, Telangana ends June with 12% rainfall deficit
content: Southwest monsoon, which runs from June to September, typically accounts for about 740.6 mm (80%) of Telangana’s annual rainfall of 923.8 mm

title: Embezzlement charges extremely serious and concerning, says Mayawati
content: Mayawati cautioned political parties that politicisation of religion is not right in the interest of the country and the people and this is also an appeal to the countrymen

title: What are India’s problems with most credit ratings agencies? | Explained
content: India has consistently been rated just a grade or two above ‘junk’ status, which is when institutions will stop lending money for fear of default

title: Why Punjab’s anti-sacrilege law has triggered a row with Akal Takht | Explained
content: Akal Takht has been annoyed over the Punjab government bringing the amendments to the law without the opinion and consent of the Akal Takht, SGPC and Sikh community

title: Congress leader's son detained, released over controversial hoardings featuring PM Modi, U.P. CM
content: The hoardings, installed at several locations between Bara Toll Plaza and the Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalaya in the Haidergarh area, have drawn widespread attention

title: Patna court extends Khan Sir's protection from arrest till July 3 in coaching institute firing case
content: Faisal Khan was named in an FIR related to a firing incident by his guards when miscreants vandalised his coaching institute in early June

title: U.S.-India relationship at its lowest in 30 years: Indian-American Democrat leader
content: Indian-American Congressman Ro Khanna claimed that during his recent visit to China, India’s Ambassador there had told him that a generation of trust was lost due to Mr. Trump’s policies

title: SIR in Karnataka: Door-to-door enumeration begins from Chief Minister’s home; D.K. Shivakumar urges voters to complete the exercise
content: The Chief Minister said the SIR process had formally begun in the State and urged voters to ensure that their details in the electoral rolls were up to date.

title: Ketan Agarwal death case: What is gait analysis? | Explained
content: In clinical settings, gait analysis can be used to understand why someone is in pain or how a specific medical condition is progressing. Read on...

title: Kerala Assembly passes resolution seeking reform of national entrance exam system, flags NEET concerns
content: In the resolution moved by Higher Education Minister, House cites serious concerns over credibility, transparency and fairness of NEET and other national-level exams

title: Delhi SIR: How to check your name in previous SIR and how to fill the enumeration form
content: As part of the process, electors must map their details with the electoral rolls prepared during Delhi’s previous revision in 2002

title: Police Sub-Inspector in Bengaluru arrested in POCSO case involving minors
content: The officer is accused of taking the moinor boys, who were detained in connection with a murder investigation, to a lodge, assaulting them and forcing them into performing obscene acts, which he recorded on his mobile phone.

title: What is EPFO’s new system | Explained
content: The EPFO is facing several technical and procedural delays in member services, such as the submission of claims for deceased members and the presentation of life certificates for pensioners

title: Switzerland’s MSC to acquire 49% stake in Adani’s Vizhinjam port in Kerala for ₹13,220 crore
content: The shipping behemoth’s move is billed as the largest foreign private investment in domestic port infrastructure in India

title: Police encounter in Bihar: Supreme Court junks plea seeking CBI probe, constitution of expert committee
content: The plea had also sought a probe by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI), claiming the case warrants a prompt, independent and impartial investigation

title: Don't care about name on letterhead: Sergio Gor on Indo-Pacific command row
content: Addressing the U.S.-India Strategic Partnership Forum Leadership Summit, U.S. Ambassador to India Sergio Gor said India still conducts more military exercises with the U.S. than any other country and has regular bilateral visits of defence officials

title: Dongria Kondh of Niyamgiri: Where forests, food and faith shape daily life
content: An intimate glimpse into the lives of the Dongria Kondh, one of India’s Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups, in Odisha’s Niyamgiri hills

title: Distribution of poor quality rice by TVK MLA: BJP demands enquiry, action over Madurai incident
content: Nainar Nagenthran was reacting to the alleged distribution of poor quality rice by Madurai West MLA S.R. Thangapandi

title: U.S., India trade deal in final steps: Sergio Gor
content: The U.S. envoy to India cited the personal equation between U.S. President Donald Trump and Prime Minister Narendra Modi which was driving the ties.

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Akasa Air, SpiceJet issue travel advisories amid adverse weather in Mumbai
content: Thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds of 30-40 kmph are likely at isolated places in Chamba, Kangra, Kullu and Mandi districts of Maharashtra throughout the week

title: Kerala Assembly: Sports Minister says only 190 playgrounds identified under One Panchayat One Playground scheme
content: O.J. Janeesh says 27 playgrounds have been completed under OPOP scheme, which is only 5% of the target

title: Monsoon intensifies across Maharashtra, Mumbai braces for heavy rain
content: IMD issues yellow alert for multiple districts; airlines caution passengers over possible flight disruptions in Mumbai and Dharamshala

title: Wars will be more integrated, theatre-oriented in future: General Dwivedi
content: General Upendra Dwivedi, an accomplished military leader with over 40 years of service who retires on June 30, was accorded a guard of honour as part of a ceremonial farewell held on the lawns of the South Block

title: Sex workers in Kolkata’s Sonagachi | Only rights can stop the wrongs, says DMSC community
content: Durbar Mahila Samanwaya Committee, a collective of sex workers, turns 30; the community talks about their lives, difficult working conditions, their long-sought demand to be recognised as workers in Kolkata

title: Why has India-U.S. trade deal been delayed? | Explained
content: From India’s Russian oil purchases to legal battles in Washington, the road to India-U.S. trade deal has become increasingly uncertain, and The Hindu explains why

title: Andhra Pradesh’s Sanjeevani health screening drew interest at national conference, says Minister
content: Citizen Health Screening will be held using 904 mobile medical units, as part of which healthcare teams will conduct 41 diagnostic tests for every individual, says Health Minister

title: AIKS calls for nationwide farmers’ protests on August 10, opposes trade agreements
content: The meeting decided that AIKS would participate in the National Convention of the Samyukta Kisan Morcha (SKM) scheduled for July 28 in Delhi

title: Fire breaks out in Haldia Petrochemicals’ naphtha pipeline, 20 injured
content: Locals say that during the early hours of the morning there was an explosion with a loud sound in the Haldia Petrochemicals pipeline.

title: UN report flags $800 billion climate fund gap in Asia-Pacific, calls for synergistic action
content: The report also highlighted conserving biodiversity through nature-positive pathways and underscored that achieving this in the Asia-Pacific region requires an enabling environment capable of supporting integrated, systemic and transformative action

title: One year on, Sigachi blast victims’ families seek justice and promised ex gratia
content: Widows, relatives of the victims and activists gathered at Pashamylaram to demand full payment of the promised ₹1 crore compensation, alleging they have received only about ₹41.5 lakh each

title: INDIA bloc writes to CJI, flags EC’s ‘biased conduct’
content: The letter was signed by 23 parties, including the AAP and DMK, which did not participate in the June 8 INDIA bloc meeting in Delhi

title: ‘It’s about time’: How a Chennai journalist’s book inspired ‘Made in India: A Titan Story’
content: Former The Hindu BusinessLine journalist Vinay Kamath reflects on seeing his book on Titan’s journey transformed into one of 2026’s streaming successes

title: Vinay Rajkumar interview: On the rollercoaster journey of ‘Gramaayana’
content: Kannada actor Vinay Rajkumar talks about his upcoming movie ‘Gramaayana’, which has been in the making for a long time.

title: Attempts to question FATF’s credibility often reflect fear of scrutiny: India at UN
content: India’s Permanent Representative to the UN Parvathaneni Harish said that countries facing adverse assessments should address the identified deficiencies, strengthen domestic enforcement, improve financial transparency, and demonstrate irreversible action against terror-financing networks

title: SIR: Citizens, citizenship, and Right to Vote
content: The door-to-door survey for Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls begins in Karnataka on June 30, amid a debate about, not the need for the process itself, but how it is going to be carried out

title: SIR: No documents needed during house-to-house enumeration process, says Karnataka Chief Electoral Officer
content: Over 5.54 crore electors to be covered in month-long house-to-house drive beginning June 30; draft rolls on August 5

title: Omar Abdullah seeks Bhagwat Mann’s intervention to stop ‘unauthorised levy on livestock transport in Punjab’
content: The J&K government has the issue examined by the Department of Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs through an internal committee.

title: Demolition drive in village near Raipur sparks protest
content: The area in question spans over 15 hectares and is situated less than five km from the Raipur airport, close to the developing administrative hub of Nava Raipur, where the Chief Minister’s residence and State Assembly are being shifted

title: Lalbagh gets modern glass restroom with QR entry and baby care facilities
content: The modern restroom features a reflective glass façade, natural skylights, adequate ventilation, CCTV surveillance at the entrance, and includes ramps and a special toilet facility for persons with disabilities and senior citizens

title: Kerala teacher booked for allegedly beating Class 2 student over slow copying
content: The incident allegedly occurred on June 16 when the student was beaten for taking too long to copy what had been written on the blackboard; Education dept launches probe

title: CCB invites complaints from private schools in alleged ₹100-crore scam
content: Based on a complaint lodged by Elangovan, the Secretary of the Federation of Associations of Private Schools in Tamil Nadu, the CCB registered a case and launched an investigation into the alleged fraud

title: Fear of devil’s number keeps some people off Mizoram’s SIR
content: According to the State’s Joint Chief Electoral Officer, these people refused to enrol or re-enrol during the Special Intensive Revision due to religious beliefs

title: Rise in food poisoning cases highlights lapses in food safety | Data
content: Nearly three-fourths of all States and Union Territories tended to have low or moderate scores on the Food Safety Index

title: Take approval from urban local body before digging roads, Yadgir DC tells officials
content: Deputy Commissioner of Yadgir district Rahul Pandve directed officials to carry out road restoration work after completion of the proposed project

title: India Crosses 107 Crore Internet Connections, 75 Crore Daily UPI Payments
content: According to the government, UPI-linked systems have already been adopted in nine countries, with more expected to join in the coming years.

title: 2 Lawyers In Siya Goyal Case And A Rs 10-Crore Defamation Suit
content: Pune-based advocate Aashuutosh Srivastava claimed Siya has appointed him as her lawyer in the Ketan Agarwal murder case.

title: Villages Cut Off, Bridges Washed Away In Monsoon Fury In Arunachal, Assam
content: Floods and landslides from monsoon rains disrupt transport and affect over 45,000 people in Arunachal Pradesh and Assam.

title: India Visit Was Among Trump's Best Bilateral Tours: Ex-US Diplomat To NDTV
content: Looking ahead to a possible visit by President Donald Trump to India next year, Juster recalled Trump's previous visit as one of the highlights of his presidency.

title: Tamil Nadu Woman Dies After Family's Wish For 'Natural Birth' At Home
content: According to investigators, Sasikala had undergone a Caesarean section (C-section) during her first pregnancy and had since developed an aversion to allopathic medicine.

title: Exiled Chinese Tycoon Jailed 30 Years In US For $1 Billion Fraud
content: Exiled Chinese tycoon Miles Guo sentenced to 30 years in US prison for a $1B fraud scheme, funding a lavish lifestyle of superyachts and penthouses.

title: Ahead Of 250th Independence Day, Trump Posts Artwork With George Washington
content: Trump shared the image on his social media platform ahead of the United States' 250th Independence Day celebrations, which will be marked on July 4.

title: Not Just Battery Range, Price: Check These Features Before Buying An EV
content: EV makers are now allocating 5-10% of their development budgets to customised features, connected technologies and customer-specific solutions.

title: Sharad Pawar's Party, Congress Merger Talks Positive, In Final Stages: Sources
content: According to sources, discussions at the senior leadership level in New Delhi over a merger of the Sharad Pawar-led NCP with the Congress are now in the final stages and have progressed positively.

title: Trump Should Be Invited As Republic Day Guest: US-India Forum Head To NDTV
content: On the proposed India-US trade agreement, Mukesh Aghi, President and CEO of the US-India Strategic Partnership Forum, said negotiators had narrowed differences substantially, with only a few issues remaining.

title: Opinion: Opinion | China's New AI Surveillance System Has A Chilling New Mission
content: A new legislation in China, which takes effect tomorrow, isn't just another security law. It reaches across borders - and into the future.

title: "He'll Be Up, He's Like Me": When Trump Wanted To Call PM Modi At 6 AM

title: How One Morning In Siberia Changed What Humanity Knows About Space
content: Around 7:15 am on June 30 in 1908, during the reign of Tsar Nicholas II, an object from space entered Earth's atmosphere over Siberia. And it changed science forever.

title: How Long Can People Stay Alive Trapped In Rubble After An Earthquake?

title: Warren Buffett Pauses Gates Foundation Donations Over Jeffrey Epstein Review
content: Warren Buffett has paused donations to the Gates Foundation pending an external review regarding the organization's ties to Jeffrey Epstein.
